name: NCR Aloha Restaurant POS API
what_it_does: NCR Aloha restaurant point-of-sale REST API for full-service restaurants, quick service, and casual dining chains to manage orders, menus, and operations through NCR's connected services platform. Enables AI agents to manage menu item configuration and pricing for restaurant menu management automation, handle order creation and modification for restaurant ordering platform integration, access table management and reservation data for full-service restaurant operations automation, retrieve transaction and sales data for restaurant analytics automation, manage loyalty program integration via NCR Loyalty for restaurant rewards automation, handle labor scheduling and time-punch integration for restaurant workforce automation, access kitchen routing and display configuration for restaurant order routing automation, retrieve inventory depletion and 86-management for restaurant inventory automation, manage online ordering integration via NCR Aloha Online Ordering module for restaurant digital ordering automation, and integrate NCR Aloha with NCR Silver, NCR Back Office, and enterprise systems for end-to-end restaurant chain management.
use_cases: Integrating third-party online orders from restaurant platform agents
Syncing menus from restaurant chain management agents
Reporting sales from restaurant analytics automation agents
Managing labor from restaurant workforce scheduling agents
not_for: Retail and non-hospitality POS (use NCR Counterpoint)
Food truck and popup low-volume operations
best_when: A full-service restaurant chain, casual dining brand, or quick service operator wanting AI agents to automate online order injection, menu synchronization, sales reporting, and labor management within NCR Aloha as the POS platform.
avoid_when: NCR DEVELOPER PARTNER PROGRAM CERTIFICATION: NCR Aloha API access requires NCR Connected Payments or NCR developer partner agreement; unauthorized third-party API integration without NCR partner certification creates NCR agreement violation and potential API access revocation. NCR CORPORATION SPINOFF TRANSITION RISK: NCR Corporation split into two companies in 2023 — NCR Atleos (ATM, financial) and NCR Voyix (retail/hospitality POS); Aloha is now part of NCR Voyix; ongoing platform consolidation and rebranding may affect API endpoint stability and documentation accuracy during transition period. ALOHA ON-PREMISE vs CLOUD API SURFACE DIFFERENCES: NCR Aloha has both traditional on-premise installation and Aloha Cloud deployment; on-premise Aloha has local API access with different behavior than Aloha Cloud REST API; automated integrations must verify deployment type (on-premise vs cloud) before API pattern selection.

auth.notes: NCR Aloha uses OAuth 2.0 through NCR developer platform. REST API with JSON. Atlanta, Georgia HQ (NCR Voyix Corporation, NYSE: VYX, spun off from NCR Corporation in 2023). NCR acquired Aloha Technologies in 2001. Most widely deployed full-service restaurant POS in US market. 150,000+ restaurant locations. NCR Connected Payments, NCR Back Office, NCR Loyalty integration. Competes with Oracle Simphony, Toast, and Lightspeed Restaurant for enterprise restaurant POS.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — per-terminal enterprise subscription for restaurant chains
pricing.paid_tiers: Per-terminal monthly subscription; enterprise negotiated pricing for chains; NCR developer partner program fees for integrators
pricing.notes: Atlanta GA. NCR Voyix (NYSE: VYX). Per-terminal enterprise subscription. NCR developer partner program required for integrators. No free tier.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth client per location; order and menu queries throttled; webhooks for order and transaction events

known_agent_gotchas: NCR VOYIX SPINOFF DOCUMENTATION AND API TRANSITION: NCR Corporation split into NCR Atleos and NCR Voyix in October 2023; Aloha POS API documentation moved from ncr.com to ncrvoyix.com; automated integrations using legacy NCR developer platform URLs may experience redirect or deprecation; verify current NCR Voyix developer platform URL before building new integrations

ON-PREMISE VS ALOHA CLOUD API BEHAVIOR: NCR Aloha on-premise restaurants use local API access with different authentication and endpoint structure than Aloha Cloud; automated integrations must detect deployment type at the location level; unified order injection automation deployed to mixed on-premise and cloud estate requires deployment-type-aware API client logic

OMNIVORE MIDDLEWARE AS ALTERNATIVE FOR MIXED POS ESTATES: Enterprises with both NCR Aloha and Oracle Simphony locations may prefer Omnivore middleware over direct Aloha API integration; automated order injection through Omnivore provides unified API regardless of underlying POS; evaluate Omnivore vs direct Aloha integration based on POS estate homogeneity

MENU CONFIGURATION PROPAGATION LEAD TIME: NCR Aloha enterprise menu configuration changes (price updates, item 86, new items) require propagation from NCR back office to POS terminals; automated menu updates submitted via API may have 15-60 minute propagation delay to terminal; implement menu propagation wait time before automated menu change confirmation to online ordering platforms

ORDER INJECTION IDEMPOTENCY RISK: NCR Aloha order injection API does not guarantee idempotency on network retry; automated order injection retry may create duplicate POS tickets; implement external order ID uniqueness check before retry to prevent duplicate kitchen tickets and duplicate charges to customers

NCR PARTNER CERTIFICATION AND SUPPORT TIERS: NCR developer partner program has tiered certification levels affecting API access scope and support SLA; automated integrations for enterprise restaurant chains should target NCR Premier Partner certification for priority support and maximum API scope; standard partner certification may have restricted API access for some enterprise Aloha modules
agent_readiness.security_notes: Restaurant POS. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA/EU. Transaction data, employee records, and restaurant operations.

security_score_components.security_notes: Restaurant POS. PCI-DSS, SOC2. OAuth2. US/CA/EU. Transaction, employee, and restaurant operations data.
